_note: Harm and misuse vector analysis: researcher's own affordance reasoning. Keyed by platform name. Never auto-generated.

affordances: Persistent contact via Snap Map / location
misuse_surface: Evidence destruction by design: content gone before it can be preserved
Solicitation and CSAM transmission with reduced forensic trail
Stranger-to-minor contact via username sharing / quick-add
Real-time coercion of imagery capture using camera-first send
Migration target: offenders move victims to Snapchat for the ephemerality
harm_vector: A child is contacted and exploited through ephemeral messaging
A child's abuse imagery is distributed or re-shared (re-victimizing the depicted child)
A child is groomed or enticed toward sexual contact or meeting

affordances: Anonymity by design (no real identity tied to account)
Pseudonymous handles, easily created and discarded
Public group/chat discovery by interest or keyword
Bot and third-party integration within chats
Image and video sharing in direct and group messages
misuse_surface: Anonymous contact with minors without traceable identity
Account churn; discard and recreate handles to evade blocks and detection
Interest-based group discovery used to locate victims or trade material
CSAM trading in direct messages and closed groups with low visibility
Offenders cite encryption/privacy as operational cover (anchorage_pd_2024_002 calls it "an encrypted chat platform")
Migration target; offenders move victims onto Kik for the anonymity layer
harm_vector: A child's abuse imagery is distributed, traded, or re-shared (re-victimizing the depicted child)
A child is contacted and exploited through anonymous messaging
A child is groomed or enticed toward sexual contact or meeting

affordances: Real-name-oriented profiles with rich personal detail
Profile creation with minimal verification (fake accounts trivial)
Search by name, school, location, interests
Friend suggestions / "People You May Know" graph traversal
misuse_surface: Fake/impersonation accounts to approach minors under false identity
Mining public profile detail (school, age, location) to target and tailor approach
Friend-network traversal to locate a victim's peers and siblings
Messenger as private channel to move contact out of public view
Building false rapport using profile-derived personal information
Migration target; initiate on Facebook, move to private/ephemeral platform
harm_vector: A child's abuse imagery is distributed or re-shared (re-victimizing the depicted child)
A child is contacted and exploited through profiles and Messenger
A child is groomed or enticed toward sexual contact or meeting

affordances: Public and private channels within servers
File and image sharing in channels and DMs
Invite-link distribution (servers spread by shareable link)
misuse_surface: Closed/private servers as low-visibility spaces to share material
Server invite-links used to gather offenders or victims into a controlled space
Real-time livestream/screen-share to direct abuse or production as it happens
Community structure used to organize and coordinate among multiple offenders
DM-to-server migration; pull a contact from public channel into private space
Pseudonymity layered with server gating to limit outside visibility
harm_vector: A child's abuse imagery is distributed, traded, or re-shared (re-victimizing the depicted child)
A child is contacted and exploited through server channels and direct messaging
A child is sextorted (coerced under threat after imagery is obtained)
A child is groomed or enticed toward sexual contact or meeting

affordances: Low-friction account creation, multiple accounts per user
misuse_surface: Public visual profiles used to identify and select minor targets
DM as direct private contact channel to a minor
Disappearing messages to reduce forensic trail during solicitation
Explore/hashtag discovery to find minors by interest or age signal
Account compromise/identity takeover to contact a victim's network under a trusted handle (ncmec_2023_430)
Migration target; move contact to ephemeral or encrypted platform
harm_vector: A child is contacted and exploited through DMs and visual profiles
A child's abuse imagery is distributed or re-shared (re-victimizing the depicted child)
A child is sextorted (coerced under threat after imagery is obtained)

affordances: Algorithmic For You feed (content surfaced by engagement, not follows)
Duet / stitch / comment interaction with strangers
Low-friction account creation, large minor user base
Profile and content visible without mutual connection
misuse_surface: Algorithmic discovery used to surface and reach minors at scale
Public videos used to identify and select targets by age signal
DM as private channel to move contact off the public feed
Comment/duet interaction to initiate contact under low suspicion
Migration target; initiate on TikTok, move to private/ephemeral platform (pa_ag_2026_004: "met on TikTok... continued to communicate")
harm_vector: A child is contacted and exploited through TikTok messaging (pa_ag_2026_004; south_florida_icac_2025_005; tbi_icac_2023_003; south_florida_icac_2024_002)
A child is groomed or enticed toward sexual contact or meeting (pa_ag_2026_004)
A child is solicited for explicit imagery through TikTok messages (south_florida_icac_2025_005; tbi_icac_2023_003)

affordances: Image-to-image manipulation of existing photos (face-swap, de-aging, nudification)
Low/no skill barrier to produce realistic synthetic media
Rapid generation at scale (many outputs, fast)
Fine-tuning on a target's images (likeness capture)
misuse_surface: Generating synthetic CSAM from text prompts (no real victim in the image) (ncmec_2024_1251: prompt-to-image program)
Manipulating a real child's benign photo into abuse imagery (nudification of an actual minor) (mt_doj_2025_001)
Chatbots used to generate CSAM from photos of children the offender knows (doj_ceos_2025_002)
Fine-tuning or prompt engineering to produce targeted synthetic abuse at scale (idaho_icac_2024_026: thousands of AI-generated images)
Chatbots used to simulate a minor, or to automate grooming conversations at scale
Voice cloning for impersonation in contact/extortion
Local model use to evade platform-side detection entirely
harm_vector: A real child's likeness or benign photo is manipulated or synthesized into abuse imagery without requiring contact during creation (mt_doj_2025_001; lapd_2025_002; ncmec_2024_164)
Synthetic CSAM is created via text prompts or chatbots without a separate traditional platform contact phase (doj_ceos_2025_002; ncmec_2024_1251; ncmec_2024_1070)
AI-generated or AI-labeled CSAM is possessed or distributed at scale under new statutory charge types (idaho_icac_2026_001; arkansas_dps_2025_005; scag_icac_2025_019)
Stated evidence N=14 (inferred N=9) in Q1 corpus — harm is production/possession-first, not contact-first (contrast TikTok/Instagram)
